title: Calochlaena blepharodes
taxonomy: Calochlaena blepharodes is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and other vascular plants. The genus Calochlaena is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae, a family of ferns. This plant is native to Australia and New Zealand. Calochlaena blepharodes is a type of fern, specifically a species of tree fern.

intro: Calochlaena blepharodes, also known as the black lipped fern, is a species of fern in the genus Calochlaena. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calochlaena blepharodes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
